spec_match.rationale: The page now maps the feature to URL IDNA processing through a live new URL() parser result and an RFC 5892 ContextJ trace for ZWJ/ZWNJ positions.

interactive.rationale: The demo has an editable domain field, preset cases, a protected-brand selector, parser and table toggles, live status panels, and a per-character table updated on input.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede promises CheckJoiners, current-browser URL parsing, ContextJ tracing, and mixed-script homograph detection, and those are the visible interactions delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: The interaction covers empty input, invalid punycode, browsers that do not yet reject the ZWJ probe, valid Virama cases, valid Persian ZWNJ, and mixed-script confusables that pass ContextJ.

references.rationale: The references section links directly to https://chromestatus.com/feature/6295810820145152 as well as the URL Standard and RFC 5892 ContextJ appendices.

design_system.rationale: Local CSS uses variables from public/styles.css, button background overrides also set color, text contrast is high, and the page is an interactive tool rather than a static card.

summary: The homograph detector now demonstrates the actual Chrome 148 IDNA ContextJ behavior: it calls the browser URL parser, explains ZWJ/ZWNJ decisions with rule-level traces, separates ContextJ blocks from visual confusable risks, and includes the required ChromeStatus reference.
